Operation 'getBrawlers' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. The current description is too minimal and lacks context about what data is returned. An AI agent needs to understand the response structure (array of objects), what fields are included, and the use case for this endpoint. The suggestion adds clarity about return format, key fields, and practical applications.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a complete list of all available brawlers in Brawl Stars. Returns an array of brawler objects containing basic information such as brawler ID, name, rarity, and class. This endpoint provides the foundational data for understanding the game's playable characters and is useful for building brawler databases or displaying brawler rosters.

message: Operation 'getBrawler' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. The current description lacks specificity about required inputs and response content. An AI agent needs to know: (1) the brawlerId parameter is required, (2) what specific data fields are returned (stats, abilities, rarity, etc.), and (3) when to use this endpoint versus the list endpoint. The suggestion provides these critical details for proper operation selection and usage.

data.current_description: Get information about a specific brawler
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information about a specific brawler by their unique brawler ID. Requires the brawlerId path parameter. Returns a comprehensive brawler object including stats (health, damage, speed), abilities, rarity classification, and other game-specific attributes. Use this endpoint when you need in-depth data about an individual brawler for analysis, comparison, or player information display.
